transportOptions.name: JR Pass (7-day)
transportOptions.costDescription: $280/person — unlimited shinkansen and JR trains nationwide
transportOptions.coverage: All JR trains including Tokyo-Kyoto-Osaka bullet trains
transportOptions.tip: Buy before arriving in Japan — not available for purchase inside Japan to foreign tourists

transportOptions.name: Suica / Pasmo Card
transportOptions.coverage: Tokyo metro, buses, convenience stores, vending machines
transportOptions.tip: Pick up at any major airport or train station immediately on arrival — cashless everywhere

transportOptions.name: Taxi
transportOptions.costDescription: $5-8 flag fall, $2/km — expensive for longer trips
transportOptions.tip: Use for late nights or with heavy luggage — generally honest, no tipping

transportOptions.name: Paris Métro
transportOptions.costDescription: €2.15/trip or €17.35 for carnet of 10 — Navigo weekly pass €30
transportOptions.coverage: Entire city including RER to Versailles and airports
transportOptions.tip: Buy a weekly Navigo pass (Monday-Sunday) if staying 4+ days — significant savings

transportOptions.name: Taxi / Uber
transportOptions.costDescription: €15-20 within central Paris, €50-70 from CDG airport
transportOptions.tip: Uber is generally cheaper than taxis; download G7 app for licensed Paris taxis

transportOptions.name: Rome Metro + Bus
transportOptions.coverage: Rome metro (2 lines) + extensive bus network
transportOptions.tip: Validate tickets before boarding — inspectors check regularly

transportOptions.name: Trenitalia / Italo (Rome-Florence)
transportOptions.costDescription: $25-45 per person each way by high-speed Frecciarossa (1h 30min)
transportOptions.coverage: Florence is 1.5hrs from Rome by high-speed train
transportOptions.tip: Book 2-4 weeks ahead for best prices — last minute is expensive

transportOptions.name: BTS Skytrain + MRT (Bangkok)
transportOptions.costDescription: 30-60 baht ($1-2) per trip, day pass 140 baht ($4)
transportOptions.coverage: Major Bangkok districts, malls, tourist areas
transportOptions.tip: BTS Rabbit Card saves time — tap in and out like Oyster. Essential for Bangkok.

transportOptions.name: Tuk-tuk
transportOptions.costDescription: 50-200 baht ($1.50-6) for short trips — always negotiate beforehand
transportOptions.coverage: Short distances in Bangkok and Chiang Mai
transportOptions.tip: Fun experience but agree on price before getting in. Metered taxis are cheaper for longer trips.

transportOptions.name: Ferry / Speedboat (Islands)
transportOptions.costDescription: $10-25 per trip between mainland and islands
transportOptions.coverage: Phuket, Koh Samui, Phi Phi, Krabi
transportOptions.tip: Overnight ferries save a night's accommodation — book through reputable operators

transportOptions.name: Barcelona Metro (TMB)
transportOptions.coverage: 10 lines covering entire city including Barceloneta beach
transportOptions.tip: T-Casual card shared among up to 10 people — great for families or groups

transportOptions.name: Aerobus (Airport Transfer)
transportOptions.costDescription: €7.50 per person each way — runs every 5-10 minutes
transportOptions.coverage: T1 and T2 terminals to Plaça Catalunya (35 min)
transportOptions.tip: Much cheaper than taxi from airport. Buy return ticket for discount.
